title: Time Based Key-Value Store
problem_statement: Implement a key-value store that can store multiple values for the same key at different timestamps and retrieve the value with the largest timestamp less than or equal to a given timestamp.

examples.input: set("foo", "bar", 1), get("foo", 1), get("foo", 3), set("foo", "bar2", 4), get("foo", 4), get("foo", 5)
examples.explanation: The store returns the most recent value at or before the requested timestamp.

constraints: At most 10^5 calls to set and get
All keys and values are lowercase strings
intuition: Keep a sorted list of timestamped values for each key and use binary search to retrieve the most recent valid entry.

approaches.name: Binary Search on Timestamped Entries
approaches.description: Store each key's values in a list sorted by timestamp. On get, binary search for the latest timestamp that does not exceed the query.
approaches.time: O(log n) for get, O(1) amortized for set
approaches.why_not: A linear scan for each get would be too slow when many timestamped values exist for a key.

interview_qa.q: How do you retrieve the correct historical value?
interview_qa.a: Binary search the sorted list of timestamped entries to find the most recent timestamp that is <= the requested timestamp.

interview_qa.q: Why store values per key in a list?
interview_qa.a: A list preserves chronological order and allows efficient binary search for historical lookups.
